methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Quantum-Systems is a Series C defense and security company founded by Florian Seibel, currently valued at €3B with $160M+ raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private companies, alongside 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The peer set includes Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D), Vannevar Labs ($1.5B, Series B), Dream Security ($1.5B, Series B), and Harmattan AI ($1.4B, Series B), with a median valuation of $1.4B and range spanning $500M to $9.2B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $160M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C to next-stage step-up multiplier, implies $272M. Peer-median benchmarking directly applies the $1.4B median from the 10-company comparable set. The stage-progression result reflects a floor anchored to capital deployed; the peer-median captures market pricing for similar-stage frontier-defense companies.

The triangulated range spans $231M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $824M. This width reflects meaningful dispersion in peer valuations and methodological differences. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier in an elevated-risk frontier-tech market, and whether Quantum-Systems' technology differentiation warrants positioning toward the peer median or below it given Series C timing.
